focus: cache + reindex + router deep: eviction/store ordering, partial chunk failure, profile-key drift

graphEvents.label: P1 router adapter cache omits embedding dimension

reviewDepthApplicability.reason: Iteration 013 rechecks cache migration/eviction, reindex batch/shard writes, and router dimension/profile isolation across committed embedding-stack code.

targetSelection.selectionReason: Prompt-selected high-risk surfaces: cache eviction/migration/profile key behavior, reindex batch loop and shard write ordering, and execution-router count+byte chunking/profile dimension isolation.

findingDetails.title: Factory-backed adapter cache ignores dimensions, so same provider/model profile swaps can reuse the wrong embedding dimension
findingDetails.claim: getEmbedderAdapter resolves dimensionsOverride but caches factory-backed adapters under provider:model only. If the same provider/model is used with a different active dimension in the same process, the router returns the old adapter/provider, while callers and cache profile keys believe they are operating under the new dimension.
findingDetails.counterevidenceSought: Checked router tests for provider/model rotation, dimension resolution, batching, and ready exposure. They assert cache keys such as openai:model-a and voyage:model-b, but do not cover two calls with the same provider/model and different dimensions. Cache lookup/storage does include profile_key and dimensions, which protects stored rows but does not force the router to construct a dimension-correct provider.
findingDetails.alternativeExplanation: Production might never switch the same provider/model between dimensions without clearing process state. The public router accepts dimensionsOverride, active profiles include dimension, and query callers derive profile keys from active dimension, so that assumption is not enforced at the boundary.
findingDetails.downgradeTrigger: Downgrade if all production callers prove same provider/model cannot be requested with different dimensions in one process, or if profile activation always calls clearEmbedderExecutionRouterState before any same-model dimension change.

searchLedger.invariant: Cache writes should enforce byte budgets after store without corrupting scoped lookup state or evicting unrelated fresh profile data first.

searchActions.result: storeEmbedding writes one scoped row, then runs global/profile/query/entry eviction in LRU order.
Tests cover global, per-profile, query budget enforcement and shrink_memory on deletion.

searchLedger.rationale: No new eviction-vs-store bug found; the post-store enforcement can delete old rows and is covered for the intended budget classes.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct read and targeted tests show scoped rows remain distinct and LRU budget trimming executes after every store.

searchLedger.invariant: Legacy and shard cache migration should preserve or backfill profile_key/input_kind so scoped lookups do not collide across profiles.

searchActions.result: Fresh migration recreates legacy cache with profile_key/input_kind and backfills profile metadata when present.
Shard migration preserves existing profile_key/input_kind when columns exist and defaults missing legacy columns to document profile empty.
Tests cover main legacy backfill and active-shard scoped cache writes/lookups.

searchLedger.rationale: No new cache migration finding; existing migration/default behavior matches legacy-compat expectations and scoped shard writes are covered.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: The risky profile/input-kind columns are either preserved/backfilled or intentionally defaulted for legacy rows, with direct tests for main backfill and shard scoped writes.

searchLedger.invariant: A failed batch should not mark the job completed or flip the active embedder unless all rows are embedded and written.

searchActions.result: The loop checks batch cardinality before writes, increments processed only after main and shard writes, and runs active-embedder/status completion in a later transaction.

searchLedger.rationale: No new partial-chunk completion bug found; failures before completion leave the job failed rather than completed or active-swapped.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Completion metadata and active embedder flip happen only after the batch loop exits normally.

searchLedger.hypothesis: The direct adapter cache may conflate same provider/model profiles that differ only by dimension.

searchActions.result: getEmbedderAdapter resolves dimensions but caches and rotates adapters under provider:model only.
Factory-backed adapter/provider captures the resolved dimensions at construction.
Query embedding derives profileKey from active dimension, obtains an adapter with embeddingDim, then stores returned embeddings under that profileKey.
Tests cover dimension resolution and provider/model rotation but no same provider/model with changed dimensions.

searchLedger.rationale: Dimension is part of the active embedding profile and cache identity, but the router cache omits it, so same-model dimension changes can reuse stale providers.

searchLedger.invariant: Router batching should split by count and bytes and throw before returning partial/null embeddings.

searchActions.result: Router builds non-empty chunks under max count and byte budget, calls embedBatch once per chunk, and throws on null slots.
Tests cover count splitting, byte splitting, order preservation, and null rejection.

searchLedger.rationale: No partial chunking bug found in the router batch assembly/return path.
searchLedger.ruledOutReason: Direct read and tests cover count cap, byte cap, output order, and null failure.

event.label: P1 router adapter cache omits embedding dimension

detail: Scope is complex; graph unavailable, so iteration used graphless fallback with configured-scope reads, exact rg, direct reads, test cross-checks, and registry dedupe.

reason: store writes scoped rows then enforces global/profile/query/entry budgets; tests cover the budget classes and shrink_memory path
main legacy migration backfills profile metadata and shard migration preserves/defaults profile_key/input_kind; scoped shard writes are tested
batch cardinality errors occur before writes, processed advances only after both main and shard writes, and active flip/status success happen after normal loop completion
router chunking has count/byte split tests plus null-slot rejection; no new partial chunk return path found
